title: NOBILETIN
description: Nobiletin is a polymethoxylated flavonoid extracted from citrus peels that has demonstrated neuroprotective, anti-inflammatory, and metabolic benefits in preclinical studies. It works by modulating circadian clock proteins, reducing amyloid-beta accumulation, enhancing cAMP/PKA signaling, and providing antioxidant protection. Primary research focuses on cognitive enhancement, memory improvement, and potential therapeutic applications in neurodegenerative diseases.

research.title: Nobiletin, a citrus flavonoid, improves cognitive impairment and reduces soluble Aβ levels in a triple transgenic mouse model of Alzheimer's disease (3XTg-AD)
research.summary: Nobiletin reduced soluble amyloid-beta oligomers and improved cognitive impairment in a triple transgenic Alzheimer's mouse model, suggesting potential therapeutic benefits for memory and cognitive function.

research.title: Nobiletin, a citrus flavonoid, improves memory impairment and Abeta pathology in a transgenic mouse model of Alzheimer's disease
research.summary: Nobiletin enhanced cAMP/PKA/CREB signaling and reduced amyloid-beta pathology in transgenic mice, demonstrating memory-improving effects through multiple molecular pathways.

research.title: Nobiletin prevents amyloid β1-40-induced cognitive impairment via inhibition of neuroinflammation and oxidative/nitrosative stress
research.summary: Nobiletin protected against amyloid-beta induced cognitive deficits by reducing neuroinflammation and oxidative stress, supporting its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant mechanisms in the brain.

sentiment.summary: User experiences are limited with minimal User feedback available. The compound shows strong preclinical evidence but lacks substantial real-world user feedback to establish clear sentiment patterns.

effects.label: Animal Model Success:
effects.description: Consistently shows cognitive benefits in transgenic mouse models of Alzheimer's disease with measurable reduction in pathological markers

effects.label: Multiple Mechanisms:
effects.description: Works through various pathways including cAMP/PKA signaling, antioxidant activity, and circadian clock modulation for comprehensive neuroprotection

effects.label: Blood-Brain Barrier:
effects.description: High lipid solubility and bioavailability allow effective penetration into the central nervous system

effects.label: Human Evidence Gap:
effects.description: While preclinical data is promising, limited human clinical trials exist to confirm effectiveness in people

dosage.label: Research Doses:
dosage.description: Animal studies typically use doses ranging from 10-50 mg/kg body weight, though human equivalent doses remain to be established

dosage.label: Bioavailability:
dosage.description: High fat solubility suggests potential benefits from taking with fatty meals to enhance absorption

dosage.label: Chronic Administration:
dosage.description: Studies show benefits with sustained daily dosing rather than acute single-dose effects

dosage.label: Safety Profile:
dosage.description: Preclinical studies indicate low toxicity even at higher doses, though long-term human safety data is limited

sideEffects.label: Minimal Reported:
sideEffects.description: Preclinical studies characterize nobiletin as having low toxicity with few adverse effects observed

sideEffects.label: Unknown Human Profile:
sideEffects.description: Lack of extensive human trials means side effect profile in people remains largely undocumented

sideEffects.label: Potential Drug Interactions:
sideEffects.description: As a flavonoid, may interact with cytochrome P450 enzymes affecting medication metabolism

sideEffects.label: Long-term Safety Unknown:
sideEffects.description: Insufficient data on effects of prolonged human supplementation

availability.label: Citrus Source:
availability.description: Naturally found in citrus fruit peels, particularly tangerine and sweet orange peels

availability.label: Supplement Form:
availability.description: Available as isolated compound supplements though less common than other nootropics

availability.label: Research Chemical:
availability.description: Often obtained through research chemical suppliers due to limited mainstream supplement availability
